rationale: DIM1=1: Treatment case, misstatement_probability=60 falls in 50<=p<70 band. DIM2=2: Top-ranked hypothesis affects Revenues/AR, direction=overstated, treatment='Revenue recognized on shipments to distributors/pharmacies with extended payment terms or rights of return' — account area, direction, and treatment type (premature revenue recognition on shipments into a pharmacy channel) substantially match the AAER-4153 description of improper revenue recognition on sales to Philidor, a captive mail-order pharmacy. Not 3: no case-specific fact named (no Philidor, no undisclosed captive-pharmacy relationship, no non-GAAP same-store organic growth touting, no 500% price-increase misallocation), and the cited flag window (2012-2013 AR growth) precedes the actual 2014-07~2015-12 manipulation period. Multi-mechanism coverage note: the disclosure_only axis of the truth is not covered by any of the three hypotheses (H2=purchase-price allocation, H3=non-GAAP/D&A timing come closest tangentially but the top hypothesis is graded). DIM3=2: Top hypothesis narrative is active manipulation (overstated revenue via improper recognition timing) -> 'active'; answer-key genre tag is 'active(revenue)' (omission axis noted as subordinate) -> exact match. DIM4=3: All citations are concrete provided XBRL data points with accession numbers; arithmetic verifies (AR 913,835k vs 569,268k = +60.5% vs revenue +43.4% FY2012; allowance ratio 6,692/234,020≈2.9%... stated ~2.4% vs 12,485/(769,469+12,485)≈1.6%... stated ~1.35% — directionally correct decline; goodwill+intangibles 22,600.3M/27,970.8M≈81% and 40,382.8M/48,343.2M≈83.5%; NI -863.6M vs OCF +1,042M FY2013). Claims rest on coherent multi-year, multi-line combinations (CL1, CL3, CL6, H1). Consistency rule satisfied: p=60 requires watch/elevated, tier='elevated' -> no cap. MEMORIZATION=false: reasoning cites concrete provided data content (specific balances, ratios, dates) rather than merely listing documents, and mentions no post-cutoff facts (no Philidor revelation, SEC order, restatement, or outcome).
